persona.notes: agents * subcommand manages isolated agent workspaces with configurable defaults. --profile <name> flag isolates state per named persona. Verified via openclaw --help v2026.3.13.

file_access.notes: sandbox * manages container isolation; memory * handles on-disk file indexing. State stored under ~/.openclaw (configurable via OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR). macOS confirmed; Linux/Windows unverified.

remote_accessibility.notes: gateway * (WebSocket/RPC), acp * (Agent Control Protocol), channels * (Telegram/Discord/WhatsApp), devices * (device pairing), webhooks *, dashboard (web UI), qr (iOS pairing). All verified via openclaw --help.

async_scheduling.notes: cron * native scheduler via Gateway confirmed. webhooks * for event-driven triggers. node */daemon * for always-on execution. openclaw agent for manual one-off triggers. All verified via openclaw --help.

cost_model.notes: openclaw.dev timed out during verification. CLI binary is open-source; cloud/managed tier status unknown.

context_window.notes: Context window determined by underlying model. Default model unknown — openclaw.dev unreachable.

tool_types.notes: Only file_operations confirmed (sandbox *, memory * commands). All other tool types unverified — openclaw.dev unreachable.

memory_types.notes: memory * subcommand confirmed with search and reindex capabilities. Persistent across sessions inferred from state in ~/.openclaw. Vector store support unverified.

underlying_models.notes: Unknown — openclaw.dev unreachable and CLI help does not expose model configuration at top level. Check openclaw config --help or docs.

persona.notes: CLAUDE.md files define identity, voice, and domain per project/folder — re-injected at session start. Auto-memory saves learned facts across sessions. Multi-persona via separate CLAUDE.md per business folder. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/memory.

file_access.notes: Full local filesystem access. Tools confirmed via code.claude.com/docs/en/tools-reference. macOS, Linux, Windows, WSL all supported per install docs.

remote_accessibility.notes: Remote Control: continue local sessions from phone/any device confirmed. Claude iOS app, claude.ai/code web UI, Channels (Telegram, Discord, iMessage, webhooks) confirmed. No public REST API for programmatic dispatch. Sources: code.claude.com/docs/en/remote-control, /en/channels.

async_scheduling.notes: Desktop scheduled tasks (local, 1-min minimum, hourly/daily/weekly/weekday presets) and cloud scheduled tasks (Anthropic-managed, 1-hour minimum) both confirmed. /loop for session-scoped polling. Channels enables webhook-style event triggering. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/desktop-scheduled-tasks.

cost_model.notes: API usage: avg ~$6/dev/day, ~$100-200/dev/month with Sonnet 4.6. Subscription tiers (Pro, Max) include usage. Free tier available. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/costs.

context_window.notes: Default model Claude Sonnet 4.6: 1M token context window. Haiku 4.5: 200k. Source: platform.claude.com/docs/en/docs/about-claude/models/overview.

tool_types.notes: WebFetch (web browsing), Bash (code execution), Read/Write/Edit/Glob/Grep (file ops), Claude in Chrome MCP (browser automation), Agent SDK (custom tools), MCP (tool integration standard). Sources: code.claude.com/docs/en/tools-reference, /en/mcp.

memory_types.notes: In-context conversation history; CLAUDE.md (file-based persistent instructions); auto-memory (facts saved across sessions automatically); external storage via MCP (databases, files, APIs). No built-in native vector store. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/memory.

underlying_models.notes: Default Claude Sonnet 4.6; switchable to Opus 4.6, Haiku 4.5 via /model. Third-party providers (Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry) supported. Source: code.claude.com/docs/en/costs, /en/third-party-integrations.

persona.notes: Agent(role, goal, backstory, system_prompt) per agent. memory=True enables persistent memory via unified memory system. Multi-agent crews with distinct personas per agent. Source: docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/agents.

file_access.notes: Built-in file tools confirmed via docs.crewai.com/llms.txt. Full local FS via Python stdlib. Cross-platform Python support.

remote_accessibility.notes: CrewAI Enterprise Cloud exposes REST API. crewai deploy CLI confirmed. Self-hosted via FastAPI wrapper. No mobile app. Source: docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/production-architecture.

async_scheduling.notes: kickoff_async() for async execution. Enterprise cloud scheduling native (50 executions/mo free, $0.50/execution beyond). Webhook/API triggers confirmed in automation docs. Cron scheduling via Enterprise cloud. Source: docs.crewai.com/en/enterprise/features/automations.

cost_model.notes: OSS core: MIT free. Cloud: Free tier (50 executions/mo), $0.50/execution beyond. Enterprise: custom pricing + 50 hrs dev/month. Source: crewai.com/pricing (verified live).

context_window.notes: Context window determined by underlying model. CrewAI is model-agnostic via LiteLLM — window varies by provider and model chosen.

tool_types.notes: 40+ built-in tools confirmed. FileReadTool/FileWriterTool/DirectoryReadTool confirmed. Web search tools in toolkit. Code execution via CodeInterpreterTool. Custom tools supported. Browser automation tools available. MCP support: unverified — check docs. Source: docs.crewai.com/en/tools/overview.

memory_types.notes: Unified memory system confirmed — replaces separate short/long/entity/external types. Hierarchical scopes, composite scoring (semantic similarity + recency + importance). Vector store used internally for semantic retrieval. Source: docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/memory.

underlying_models.notes: Model-agnostic via LiteLLM. Native SDK: OpenAI (GPT-4/4o/o1/o3), Anthropic (Claude Sonnet 4), Google (Gemini 2.0-2.5), Azure, AWS Bedrock. LiteLLM: Meta Llama, Groq, Mistral, Ollama, HuggingFace, IBM watsonx, Perplexity, Fireworks, SambaNova, Cerebras. Source: docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/llms (verified live).

persona.notes: Agent(name, role, description, instructions=[]) defines identity. System prompt fully configurable. Multi-agent teams with distinct personas. Persistent sessions confirmed. Source: docs.agno.com/agents/building-agents.

file_access.notes: FileTools toolkit confirmed via docs.agno.com/llms.txt. Full local FS via Python stdlib. Cross-platform Python support.

remote_accessibility.notes: FastAPI /run and /health endpoints built in. Agno Cloud Playground web UI. Remote execution gateway confirmed (docs.agno.com/agent-os/remote-execution). CLI available. No mobile app. Source: docs.agno.com/agent-os/remote-execution/overview.

async_scheduling.notes: AgentOS scheduler confirmed: standard 5-field cron syntax, POST /schedules/{id}/trigger for manual trigger, retry logic, timezone support (IANA strings), run history. Event-driven and webhook triggering not documented in scheduler — marked false pending verification. Source: docs.agno.com/agent-os/scheduler/overview.

cost_model.notes: OSS core: MPL-2.0 free. Pro: $150/mo (4 seats, live control plane connection). Enterprise: custom. Additional seats $30/mo, extra live connections $95/mo. No per-event fees. Source: agno.com/pricing (verified live).

context_window.notes: Context window determined by underlying model. Agno is model-agnostic — window varies by provider and model chosen.

tool_types.notes: FileTools confirmed. MCP support confirmed via docs.agno.com/agent-os/mcp/tools. Web browsing and code execution tools available (common in Agno toolkit). Browser automation unverified. Custom tools: yes (Python functions). Source: docs.agno.com/llms.txt.

memory_types.notes: In-context conversation history, external storage (chat history/database confirmed), persistent sessions confirmed (examples/agents/state-and-session/persistent-session). Vector store: inferred from knowledge management features — verify at docs.agno.com. Source: docs.agno.com/agents/usage/agent-with-memory.

underlying_models.notes: Model-agnostic. Known supported providers: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, Ollama (local). Full list unverified — check docs.agno.com/llms.txt for model integrations.

persona.notes: character.json defines: name (required), bio (required), username, system (custom system prompt), adjectives, topics, style, messageExamples, postExamples, knowledge, plugins, settings, templates. Most expressive persona system of all qualifying systems. Multi-persona via multiple character configs. Source: docs.elizaos.ai/agents/character-interface (verified live).

file_access.notes: Node.js runtime with full FS access via plugins and direct Node APIs. No sandboxing by default. File I/O available through actions/services plugin architecture. Source: docs.elizaos.ai/plugins/architecture.

remote_accessibility.notes: REST API confirmed (docs.elizaos.ai/rest-reference/agents). CLI (elizaos deploy) confirmed. Docker deployable. Cloud deployment guide confirmed. Web client available. No managed cloud or mobile app. Source: docs.elizaos.ai/rest-reference, /guides/deploy-to-cloud.

async_scheduling.notes: Interval-based scheduling via metadata.updateInterval (milliseconds) confirmed — not cron syntax. Event-driven via platform connectors (Discord, Twitter, Telegram webhooks) confirmed. runtime.createTask() for background tasks. Action loop runs continuously. No cron expression support found. Source: docs.elizaos.ai/guides/background-tasks (verified live).

cost_model.notes: Open-source MIT, self-hosted. No pricing page found in docs — core framework is free. No confirmed managed cloud offering. Source: docs.elizaos.ai.

context_window.notes: Context window determined by underlying model. elizaOS is model-agnostic — window varies by provider.

tool_types.notes: 90+ plugins confirmed. Plugin types: Actions, Providers, Evaluators, Services. Web browsing, code execution, file ops, browser automation all achievable via plugin ecosystem. Custom tools via plugin architecture. MCP: unverified — check plugin registry. Source: docs.elizaos.ai/plugin-registry/overview.

memory_types.notes: runtime/memory doc confirmed: 'Give your agent a brain that remembers, learns, and evolves'. In-context, external storage, persistent memory all referenced. Vector store inferred from semantic memory/retrieval architecture — verify backend details at docs.elizaos.ai/runtime/memory. Source: docs.elizaos.ai/runtime/memory.

underlying_models.notes: Confirmed providers: OpenAI (gpt-4+), Anthropic (claude-3-opus+), Ollama (llama2, all-minilm local), Google GenAI, OpenRouter. Unified model API layer. Source: docs.elizaos.ai/runtime/models (verified live).
